title: 10 best herbal tinctures for gum swelling

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with gum swelling because they contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and ease swelling in the gums. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice also has antibacterial and antiviral effects, which can help to combat infections that may be contributing to gum swelling. Additionally, the soothing and calming nature of licorice can help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums. By reducing inflammation and combating infection, herbal licorice tinctures can provide effective relief from gum swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal licorice tincture contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which reduces inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, making it an effective remedy for gum swelling.

Antioxidant properties: Licorice root contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to gum inflammation and damage, allowing herbal licorice tincture to promote a healthy oral environment.

Soften and protect mucous membranes: Herbal licorice tincture's triterpenoid saponins have been shown to soften and protect mucous membranes, including those in the mouth, by reducing inflammation and irritation, which can help alleviate gum swelling and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice root contains a wealth of bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, making it an effective ingredient in tinctures for gum care.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots at 20-30% moisture content for optimal extraction.
Cut the dried roots into small pieces and soak them in 80-proof vodka or everclear at 1:4 ratio.
Steep the root pieces in the vodka mixture for 14 days, shaking daily to ensure maximum extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a glass bottle, discarding the solids and retaining 20 ounces of liquid.
Store the herbal licorice tincture in a cool dark place at room temperature, using it within 6 months of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated gums. The tannins present in pot marigold work to reduce redness and swelling, while its flavonoids and phenolic acids help combat free radicals that can exacerbate gum inflammation. By applying the tincture topically or taking it orally, individuals can alleviate gum pain and discomfort, promoting a healthy and comfortable oral environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tincture contains calenchoside, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, including in the gums.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tincture, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing gum swelling and inflammation.

Wound-healing and antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold tincture has been traditionally used to promote wound healing and prevent infection, which can be beneficial in cases of gum disease or injury where the gums are swollen and inflamed.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce gum inflammation and swelling.
Seeds: The seeds contain essential fatty acids and omega-3 fatty acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe gum swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula officinalis flowers at peak blooming stage and dry them thoroughly in a well-ventilated area.
Combine one part dried calendula officinalis flowers with 5 parts vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure uniform extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Transfer the final tincture to small amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, labeling and dating each bottle for future use within two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce the redness and puffiness associated with swollen gums. The tannins present in dandelion root help to constrict blood vessels, thereby reducing bleeding and inflammation. Additionally, dandelion's antimicrobial properties combat the underlying bacterial infections that often cause gum swelling, promoting a healthy oral environment and alleviating discomfort and pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, providing relief from conditions like gingivitis and periodontal disease.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of dandelion tincture can help protect the gum tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and swelling.

Bicarbonate and alkalinity: Dandelion's natural bicarbonate content and slightly alkaline pH can help neutralize acidic environments in the mouth, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy balance of oral flora.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing gum inflammation and swelling.
Buds: Contain bioactive compounds that can help soothe and calm irritated gums, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and chop them into small pieces for a total of 2 cups.
Combine the chopped dandelion roots with 4 cups of vodka and 2 cups of water in a clean glass jar.
Stir the mixture well and store it in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Store the resulting tincture in the refrigerator for up to 2 years and take 20-30 drops per day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm inflamed tissues. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that reduces inflammation and prevents further irritation. Additionally, lemon balm's antiseptic and antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial growth and infections that may contribute to gum swelling. As a result, herbal lemon balm tinctures provide natural relief from gum swelling, promoting healthy gums and reducing discomfort associated with gingivitis and periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, which can contribute to swelling.
Calming and soothing properties: Lemon balm has a calming effect on the mucous membranes, including those in the mouth and gums, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort.
Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm's antimicrobial effects can help combat bacterial infections that may contribute to gum swelling and inflammation, such as periodontitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, lemon balm roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce gum inflammation and swelling.
Seeds: Containing a significant amount of fatty acids, lemon balm seeds possess natural antibacterial and antifungal properties to combat gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn or late afternoon for maximum potency.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves by gently brushing off excess dirt and moisture.
Chop the dried leaves into fine pieces to increase surface area and facilitate extraction.
Combine 250g of chopped lemon balm with 750ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar, then seal tightly.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily to enhance tincture production.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins and phenolic acids present in the herb have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen gums. Additionally, the antimicrobial compounds found in Kansas coneflower have been proven effective against bacteria that can cause periodontal disease, which often leads to gum swelling. By targeting both the inflammation and bacterial causes of gum swelling, Kansas coneflower tinctures provide a natural solution for promoting healthy gums and oral tissues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The tannins, flavonoids, and other compounds in Kansas coneflower tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Antimicrobial: The antibacterial and antifungal properties of Kansas coneflower tincture can help combat infections that can cause gum inflammation and swelling, promoting a healthy oral environment.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum swelling.
Seeds: Containing mucilages and saponins that can help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia flowers and leaves in quantities of 1 part material to 2 parts vodka.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and mix it with vodka in a glass jar, filling it about three-quarters full.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the plant material and reserving the tincture.
Transfer the tincture to smaller dark glass bottles and store it in the refrigerator at 2-8 degrees celsius for up to two years, taking 15-30 drops twice daily as needed for gum swelling relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that reduces inflammation and relieves pain. When applied topically to the gums, it helps to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort caused by gum infections or irritation. Additionally, St John's wort's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial growth in the mouth, further contributing to its effectiveness in soothing swollen gums and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort's antioxidant compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, which can contribute to gum swelling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in St John's Wort tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation and infections.

Antibacterial properties: St John's Wort's antimicrobial compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, can help combat bacterial growth and infection in the gums, which can cause swelling and other symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort are used to make medicinal tinctures for gum swelling due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Barks: The barks of St John's Wort contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective against gum inflammation and infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers at dawn on a sunny day.
Rinse the plant material gently with distilled water and pat dry with a clean cloth.
Combine 50g of dried st john's wort herb with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar and stir well.
Store the mixture in a dark place for 14 days, shaking daily to extract the active compounds slowly.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet cicely tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which work to reduce redness and pain associated with swollen gums. The tincture's natural ingredients, including ferulic acid and saponins, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing. remedy_parts: Roots: Sweet cicely roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing gum swelling and alleviating pain.
Seeds: The seeds of sweet cicely contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh myrrhis odorata leaves and stems on a sunny morning.
Clean and chop the harvested material into small pieces weighing approximately one pound.
Combine the chopped plant material with 80% vodka in a clean glass jar filling it almost full.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6 weeks shaking gently every other day for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums. Wound-healing: Comfrey's wound-healing properties can help accelerate the repair of damaged tissues in the mouth, including the gums, reducing the appearance of swelling and promoting overall oral health.

Buds: High in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in combating gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh comfrey leaves and stems at the peak of bloom, selecting those free from damage or disease.
Chop the harvested comfrey material into small pieces to facilitate extraction and increase surface area.
Combine 1 part chopped comfrey with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a dark place for at least six weeks, shaking occasionally, for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids, and label the tincture with date and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in yarrow, such as achillein and sesquiterpenes, work to reduce inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried yarrow leaves flowers and stems in late summer early fall when essential oils are most potent.
Chop the yarrow plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 100g of chopped yarrow with 500ml of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar and stir well.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 6-8 weeks shaking jar daily to extract constituents slowly.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids and label date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and puffiness in the gums. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh chamomile flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Rinse the flowers gently with filtered water and pat dry with paper towels.
Combine one part dried chamomile flowers with one part high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining the liquid and discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store at room temperature, ready to use as needed.

Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks allowing it to steep and extract the active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eupatorium perfoliatum leaves and stems in the morning after the dew has dried.
Chop 1 cup of fresh or 2 cups of dried eupatorium perfoliatum plant material into small pieces using scissors or a herb cutter.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 cups of high-proof vodka or other neutral spirits in a clean glass jar and cover it with a lid.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks at room temperature, shaking the jar daily to promote extraction.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture with the date and ingredients.

Combine 100g of the harvested plant material with 500ml of vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks allowing it to macerate and extract.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems, rinsing them gently with cold water.
Chop the plant material into small pieces, ensuring all parts are well broken down.
Combine 250 grams of chopped plant material with 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of cymbopogon citratus in the morning after dew has evaporated and clean them gently with distilled water.
Combine 200 grams of cleaned cymbopogon citratus leaves with 400 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for eight weeks, shaking it daily to ensure uniform extraction of essential oils and bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid from the plant material using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining 300 milliliters of tincture.
Transfer the 300-milliliter tincture to dark glass dropper bottles and label them with date prepared dosage instructions and intended use for oral administration as a remedy for gum swelling.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality curcuma longa roots and clean them thoroughly removing any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned curcuma longa roots in a warm, dry place for 24-48 hours before cutting into small pieces.
Combine one part dried curcuma longa root with five parts vodka in a clean glass jar filling it to the top.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks or longer allowing for maximum extraction of curcuminoids.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids before transferring the tincture into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to inhibit prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger rhizomes carefully selecting roots that are free of mold and mildew for optimal quality.
Clean the harvested ginger thoroughly by rinsing under cold running water removing any dirt or debris.
Slice the cleaned ginger into thin pieces approximately 1/8 inch thick to increase surface area for tincture production.
Combine the sliced ginger with a neutral grain spirit such as vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of one part ginger to two parts spirit and seal tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks shaking the jar daily allowing the ginger to steep and extract its active compounds.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian blue gum plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums.
Bark: The bark of the plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from mature plants during the peak growing season.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves by gently shaking off excess moisture and spreading them out in a single layer.
Combine the dried leaves with 200 milliliters of 95% ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the plant material is completely submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction, allowing the tincture to mature slowly.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in the gums. The tincture contains compounds like histamine and acetylcholine that help constrict blood vessels, thereby decreasing swelling and bleeding. Additionally, stinging nettle's natural antiseptic and antibacterial properties work to combat gum infections, promoting a healthy and pain-free environment for the teeth and surrounding tissues. By using herbal stinging nettle tinctures, individuals can experience relief from gum swelling and improve their overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stinging nettle tincture contains compounds that reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate swelling and pain in the gums.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in stinging nettle tincture help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy oral environment.

Anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating flavonoids: Stinging nettle's flavonoid compounds have been shown to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and modulate the immune response, helping to reduce gum swelling and prevent further inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Stinging nettle roots have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and ease pain in oral mucosa, making them a suitable choice for treating gum swelling.
Seeds: Nettle seeds contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe irritated gums and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers in the early morning for maximum potency.
Rinse the plant material thoroughly with filtered water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the harvested leaves and flowers into small pieces using scissors or a food processor.
Mix 10 parts of chopped plant material with 20 parts of high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for gum swelling are liquid extracts made from plants, roots, and herbs that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These tinctures help reduce gum inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with swollen gums by soothing the affected area and promoting healthy tissue growth. Examples of herbal tinctures that aid in gum swelling relief include calendula, chamomile, and sage, which can be used as a mouthwash or gargle to ease symptoms. By using these tinctures, individuals with gum swelling can enjoy improved oral health, reduced pain and discomfort, and increased confidence in their overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum swelling with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial herbs. Apply a few drops of tincture made from ginger, chamomile, or calendula directly to the affected area several times a day. Alternatively, use a mouthwash made from peppermint, sage, or echinacea to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria. Regular use can help alleviate swelling and discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum swelling that are treated with herbal tinctures are inflammatory conditions such as arthritis, joint pain, and skin irritations. Herbal tinctures like turmeric, ginger, and willow bark contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain. Additionally, tinctures made from herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and calendula can soothe and calm irritated tissues, making them effective treatments for conditions like mouth sores, burns, and eczema.
